channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Élevé] CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems.
Unlike other information stealers that are built on…

item.title: [Faible] Google and Microsoft Pull ModHeader With 1.6 Million Installs After Dormant Collector Found
item.description: Google and Microsoft have pulled ModHeader, a popular header-editing extension with roughly 1.6 million installs across Chrome and Edge, after researchers found a hidden browsing-history collector built into its…

item.title: [Faible] Russian celebrity journalist Ksenia Sobchak says hackers accessed Telegram channels via email breach
item.description: Following the breach of several of her Telegram channels, controversial Russian journalist Ksenia Sobchak claimed published screenshots of her correspondence with political figures were fake.

item.title: [Critique] CISA warns of actively exploited RCE flaws in Joomla extensions
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the iCagenda and Balbooa Forms extensions for Joomla to achieve remote code execution through…

item.title: [Critique] ⚡ Weekly Recap: ShareFile Threat, Citrix Bleed 2 Ransomware, AI Coding Attacks, and More
item.description: Somewhere right now, a security tool is quietly finding bugs faster than any human can fix them. That's supposed to be the good news. The catch is that the attackers have the same tools, pointed the other way, and they…

item.title: [Faible] Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub…

item.title: [Faible] Lidl discloses online shop breach after service provider hack
item.description: German discount supermarket chain Lidl notified customers in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands that attackers stole their personal information in a breach at a service provider. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Breach at the Beach: Play the Ultimate Entra ID CTF
item.description: Learn how attackers abuse Entra ID through a free hands-on Capture the Flag. Varonis created the Breach at the Beach CTF to teach defenders how to investigate Entra ID attack techniques using realistic scenarios. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New MemGhost Attack Plants Persistent False Memories in AI Agents Through One Email
item.description: Give an AI assistant a memory and access to your inbox, and you hand an attacker a way to rewrite what it thinks it knows about you. A single email can trick that agent into saving a false "fact" about the user, hide…

item.title: [Faible] UK charges suspects linked to Russian Coms call spoofing platform
item.description: UK authorities charged five people following a National Crime Agency (NCA) investigation into Russian Coms, a major caller ID spoofing platform used by criminals to make over 1.8 million scam calls. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted lure creation,…

item.title: [Faible] UK and Allies urge critical sectors to improve defences against Russian intelligence targeting
item.description: New advisory highlights Russian state cyber actors’ global exploitation of poorly configured routers

item.title: [Faible] Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
item.description:
Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every read.
Each read gets…

item.title: [Faible] Thinking Fast and Slow in the SOC: The Case for Combining Autonomous AI with Analyst Copilots
item.description: A few days ago, I was sitting with the CISO of a Fortune 50 company, walking through how his security team was thinking about AI agents in the SOC. Smart team. Serious program. They had already connected Claude to a few…

item.title: [Faible] EU sanctions Russian GRU military hackers over cyberattacks
item.description: The European Union and the United Kingdom jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities and accused Russia of coordinating a network of hacking groups responsible for attacks across Europe. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an intrusion in which an unknown threat actor leveraged a vibe-coded PowerShell script for Active Directory (AD) enumeration.
"The script looked for the Domain Controller (DC) and…

item.title: [Faible] US and allies warn of Russian critical infrastructure attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity agencies from the United States and eight other countries have issued a joint warning that Russian state hackers are targeting vulnerable and poorly configured routers to infiltrate critical infrastructure…

item.title: [Faible] Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
item.description: An attacker running a live Microsoft 365 phishing operation left a Python web server listening on a public port with directory listing switched on. The command that did it: python3 -m http.server 8080, was still…

item.title: [Critique] iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two maximum-severity security flaws impacting iCagenda and Balbooa extensions for Joomla to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog,…

item.title: [Faible] OpenAI temporarily relaxes GPT-5.6 Sol usage limits
item.description: OpenAI is temporarily relaxing GPT-5.6 Sol usage after demand for the company's most powerful model surged over the past 48 hours. [...]

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2008-4128 — Cisco IOS Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability
item.description: Cisco IOS 12.4 contains multiple cross-site forgery vulnerabilities that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via (1) a certain "show privilege" command to the /level/15/exec/- URI, and (2) a certain "alias exec" command to the /level/15/exec/-/configure/http URI.

item.title: [Faible] Russia's FSB blamed for Poland grid attack as UK and EU impose first joint cyber sanctions
item.description: The allies blamed Center 16, the FSB’s signals intelligence arm, for acts of attempted cyber sabotage targeting Poland’s energy sector and water treatment facilities, alongside “a wide range of malicious cyber…

item.title: [Faible] Claude Fable 5 stays free for paid users until July 19 as Anthropic buys more time
item.description: Anthropic has just extended access to Claude Fable 5 for paid subscribers until July 19, giving you another week to keep using the most powerful model. [...]
